item.content:encoded: Real Scranton hours

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep. Did not see that coming

item.title: Re: I want to make sure I understand this
item.content:encoded: Ah that makes much more sense. Thank you!

item.title: Re: I want to make sure I understand this
item.content:encoded:
The way I read it, originally it was contained in a steel room, inside a larger concrete room, with at least one Agent on site, and multiple squads of hitmen. As time went on, the specifics of containment in its mind began to blur together and all it knew was that it was contained in a room with people watching it. Because the nature of this reality altering skip is, "what it believes becomes real," the steel and concrete rooms actually combined into one room and the people themselves started to meld together. As this happens, the skip began to gain confidence in its ability to escape and began to believe more and more in its superiority and more and more in the inferiority of the Foundation site. Compared to itself, it sees the Foundation site as shortsighted and inept. Finally, it sees the Foundation site as nothing more than group of children automata mindlessly attacking whatever happens to wander into their midst. And that's what they become.

And no, it's not contained anymore, it's long gone.

item.title: I want to make sure I understand this
item.content:encoded:
+ My Thoughts
- close

So the Foundation had some sort of underwater site dedicated to containing… something. We know it's sapient and capable of reality altering to match its perception of the world around it, but that's basically it. The scip then starts breaching containment by I guess affecting the Agent and the structural integrity of the site itself. Knowing the risks of uncontrolled reality altering, the Foundation stores the scip's documentation inside a Scranton Box to make sure it isn't completely wiped and then… something happens. I guess the twenty-six statues are the previous personnel of the site and were converted into statues by the previous scip. What I'm wondering though is this: Is the scip in the center of the ring of statues? Did it escape and is currently uncontained? Was it neutralized? And another thing: the last part of the Addendum mentioned how the Agent and the walls of the chamber "melded" with the scip. Does anybody understand what's going on there or does it simply mean that the scip is just screwing with the physical properties of its containment? I just wanted to make sure I wasn't missing anything crucial to understanding the concept of the SCP.

This SCP will always be my favorite on the site for years to come. Leaves SO much to be imagined. Enthusiastic +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I read this before and was a little 'eh' on it, but on re-read I quite like it. (Possibly I saw an earlier draft, actually.) +1'd, either way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So this is the origin of all the Scranton Reality Anchoring stuff we see today on this site?

Only took me a solid year after I first read this to finally understand it. Wow. Just wow.

Then I saw the +99. I had to. It was my destiny (as a newbie, I suppose). +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Sunken Children's Perimeter Foundation. +1.

item.title: Image Attribution
item.content:encoded: The images in this article are by Jason de Caires Taylor, and are used here with his permission.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I… don't…quite… get it… yet I upvoted anyway…

I came here from the discussion page on SCP-2000 so I'm interpreting the last addendum through that lense, but without it, I wouldn't quite get it…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When I read the draft, they actually Krav Maga'd things to death. I said it's silly then, and it doesn't become less silly because the martial arts are nonspecific now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But they don't have D-class to throw at things in order to utilize all the adaptability the foundation has.

may just result in two of the statues lunging out to grab it together

Do they tear things into pieces of < 40 grams and lose interest afterwards?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As far as I understood from the article, it is some sort of a reality warper that warped the containment, the guards, and the poor sod it warped, into one thing, and is currently on the loose.

Doesn't make underwater karate less silly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with Ambrosius that there could be a slight clarification as to where and when the materials were recovered, but i really enjoyed this. The ominousness in the document is fun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're assuming we know the facility was lost. Remember, we're talking about a reality bender there. We may not have so much lost a facility, as suddenly never had one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ok, so this was once something owned by the foundation/controlled by the foundation. So my question is, why haven't the loss of an entire facility been reported? Why do we have to refer to a half damaged document? Shouldn't the foundation keep track of things like this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Something that kinda rubbed me the wrong way was the "partial manifest of recovered materials" collapsible. And by that I mean: materials recovered from where? And how would they know that the document was relevant to this SCP? But that aside, I really enjoyed this. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Forgot to post this earlier.

Thanks to Rights for helping me come up with this idea (in 2011), thanks to everyone who reviewed this for me in the past… ever… and thanks to TheRaven, Jason deCaires Taylor (the original creator) and Faminepulse for the pictures.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel that maybe you didn't fully comprehend the Addendum.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My screen is much thinner than that. Didn't think that'd be an issue.

Fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It looks like this at that resolution. At my default resolution (1600 x 900), it looks like this. You could probably lose the "Addendum:", though — I assume that's what Carbo's talking about.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a bigger story here, dear. Note how manythere are. They all seem to practice Asian martial arts. Are these the Eight Immortals split once more?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They use multiple techniques, depending on what the target is. A large fish may get underwater-karate'd, whereas a blob of garbage may just result in two of the statues lunging out to grab it together.

Remember, these were Foundation personnel. They're adaptable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1451 have been observed utilizing multiple different tactics when neutralizing a target, including multiple martial arts forms, pressure point application (to humans and machines), and highly coordinated teamwork.

So when an object with a mass of 41 g and up comes into a circle, statues destroy it with underwater karate.

Far too silly to upvote for a serious article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mind giving me a screenshot? I may be able to fix it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gotta admit that I found that a bit irksome also, but I suppose in cases like these we can assume nothing o3o dat upvote tho

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Although the "OVERSEER CLEARANCE REQUIRED" heading still looks weird on 1366x768…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
@Denizin/@Ketaren

Fixed, sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i am the subtlest at laying easter eggs

>_>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fun fact: I was the one who gave him the idea for those boxes.

I'm glad with how this SCP turned out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a great exercise in providing just enough information to be both interesting and mysterious, while the skip itself provides a nicely creepy backdrop. Great work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Quite possibly then! :O

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I started writing this in 2011! It's very likely that you've seen an incarnation of this article if you've been a denizen of chat during select weeks in the past two years.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Object itself is a little dull, but the implications of the document are great.

That said, is this a repost? I swear I've seen this before.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Minor quibble: "multiple different tactics when neutralizing a target, including mixed martial arts … etc"

This is somewhat akin to saying "playing tennis using multiple different tactics, including tennis, serving, and teamwork" (I appreciate that pressure points aren't seen in mixed martial arts competitions due to their low efficacy, but I trust the point remains that 'mixed martial arts' doesn't describe a type of tactic itself, but a sport under which many 'tactics' may be used in various combinations)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

First read through I was disappointed. I loved the picture, read it through, and thought, "What? Just a bunch of metal statues that hit things?" Then I read the found document. I like the scranton box (Haven't we seen that before?) and was confused. Came back later and I've read it a few times and I think I get it, and I really like it. Great job.

My only complaint is this sentence: "This is the ideal state for SCP-1451 to be in for containment." This is clunky and should be reworded. Maybe "This is the ideal containment state." or even just "This is the ideal state."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Question. What's the object's name? Or is it deliberately [ACCESS DENIED]?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In the text box below the second picture, did you mean to put preceding in place of proceeding?

Otherwise, an interesting read. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow… My stomach is turning over how badly we've already lost this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't complain too much Proc, at least the AUthor didn't use a [DATA EXPU[DATA LOST].

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Item #: S[DATA LOST][DATA LOST]ject Class: Ket[DATA LOST]Special Contai[DATA LOST]ures:

Attempts to decipher this have baffled Foundation cryptologists.
Snark aside, I like it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted for Scantron Scranton boxes. I love the idea, it's wonderfully reusable, and it goes a long way to making the final document work for me.

Since it's a former foundation site at the bottom of the sea with reality-bending attached, I can't help but imagine that this is somehow related to the Marianas Trench incident - maybe this (or something like it) was what they used to reset the timeline, but they had to give up an entire site to cram it back into its bottle afterwards.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this one. Well played.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: After another look, upvoted for the implications that there's some horrible intangible entity inside the circle.
